ddls:
CREATE TABLE "subpopulations" (
  "subpopulation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subpopulation */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the subpopulation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subpopulation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subpopulation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genetic_data" (
  "genetic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genetic data entry */,
  "subpopulation_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the subpopulation */,
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the population */,
  "Alleles" INTEGER /* Number of alleles in the subpopulation */,
  "He" REAL /* Heterozygosity of the subpopulation */,
  "Ho" REAL /* Observed heterozygosity of the subpopulation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genetic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (researcher, data analyst, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission granted to the user (view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each research team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the research team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the research team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_members" (
  "member_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team member */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the research team */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("member_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data usage entry */,
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the population */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of data access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each genetic data entry
Number of alleles in the subpopulation
Role of the user (researcher, data analyst, administrator)
Permission granted to the user (view, edit, delete)
Unique identifier for each research team
Unique identifier for each team member
Foreign key referencing the research team
Unique identifier for each data usage entry
